title: AI search agents stumble when questions are ambiguous
friendlyTitle: AI search tools need to ask better questions
summary: The Decoder reports on DiscoBench, a benchmark from Tencent Hunyuan and Tsinghua University showing that search agents often fail not because they cannot search, but because they do not ask clarifying questions when user requests are vague or wrong. Even strong models stayed below 50% end-to-end accuracy on ambiguous multi-step tasks.
friendlySummary: A new benchmark found that AI search agents often go wrong when your request is unclear. Instead of asking a follow-up question, they may guess and keep searching in the wrong direction.
whyItMatters: For daily use, the lesson is immediate: agentic search tools need permission to pause and ask questions instead of confidently guessing. Teams evaluating research agents should measure clarification behavior, not just retrieval speed or answer polish.
friendlyWhyItMatters: When you use AI for research, it is safer to let it ask you questions. A small clarification can prevent a long, confident, wrong answer.
